chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions and context for installing the Rheonics SRV inline viscometer EX without a temperature connection, explaining when temperature input is not required and referencing relevant diagrams from the installation manual.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863
SRV inline viscometer EX installation without temperature
Modified on: Wed, 15 May, 2024 at 10:07 PM
SRV EX Installation without temperature
The SRV can be used without a temperature connection to the electronics. Operation of sensor does not depend on the temperature and hence a temperature input is not required.
the SRD needs temperature input to the electronics so either the temperature sensor built in the sensor is used or an external Pt1000 can be connected to sensor electronics. It is also possible to write temperature of the sensor from an external probe over Modbus.
This article focuses on using SRV without a temperature connection. This could be the case when the EX barriers that are needed for Pt1000 are not available or the customer does not want to use 2 additional barriers that are needed for the Pt1000 connection.
Ex Installation diagram without temperature
The figure below shows the diagram for the connection. It is taken from the EX installation manual (Figure 10 from Manual version 2.0 of manual).
key_facts: The SRV viscometer can be installed and operated without a dedicated temperature connection to its electronics.
The operation of the SRV sensor is independent of temperature, thus eliminating the need for a temperature input.
The SRD device requires a temperature input, which can be sourced from a built-in sensor, an external Pt1000 probe connected to the sensor electronics, or via Modbus from an external probe.

This document specifically addresses SRV installations without temperature connections, particularly when Pt1000 EX barriers are unavailable or when avoiding two additional barriers for Pt1000 connections is desired.
A connection diagram for this installation method is provided in the EX installation manual as Figure 10 (from Manual version 2.0 of manual).
diagram_labels: Figure 10 from Manual version 2.0 of manual

Zener barrier specification
The Zener diode barrier connected to the transducer coil circuit must meet the following specifications (Table 4 from EX installation manual version 2.0):

Transducer coil circuit Zener diode barrier specifications
Max. output voltage Uo < 7.3 V
Min. series resistance Ro > 9.8 Ohm
Max. output current Io < 730 mA
Max. output power Po < 1.3 W
Fuse rating <=200 mA

This Zener diode barrier must have two channels, one for each conductor of the transducer coil circuit.

Commercial Zener diode barriers that fulfill these specifications are inTable 6 from EX installation manual version 2.0:
For transducer coil circuit:
key_facts: The Zener diode barrier connected to the transducer coil circuit must meet specific electrical specifications for maximum output voltage, minimum series resistance, maximum output current, maximum output power, and fuse rating.
The Zener diode barrier must have two channels, one for each conductor of the transducer coil circuit.
Commercial Zener diode barriers that fulfill these specifications are referenced in Table 6 from EX installation manual version 2.0.
diagram_labels: M12 cable connector female, rear view
warnings_notes: The Zener diode barrier connected to the transducer coil circuit must meet the following specifications (Table 4 from EX installation manual version 2.0).
This Zener diode barrier must have two channels, one for each conductor of the transducer coil circuit.
Commercial Zener diode barriers that fulfill these specifications are inTable 6 from EX installation manual version 2.0.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides specifications for a Pepperl + Fuchs Z757 Zener diode barrier, explains notes on temperature output for Rheonics sensors, and offers references to related manuals and articles.
chunks.extracted_text:
1. Pepperl + Fuchs Z757

Specification of commercial Zener diode barrier suitable for transducer coil circuit

| Series resistance | 9.8 ohms, min. |
|---|---|
| Fuse rating | 200 mA |
| Maximum safe voltage | 250 V |
| Output voltage, U₀ | 7.14 V |
| Output current, I₀ | 729 mA |
| Output power, P₀ | 1.3W |

Notes on temperature output

Even though there is no direct measurement of temperature from the probe when the temperature leads from the probe are not connected to the electronics, the sensor does estimate a process temperature based on the frequency characteristics of the sensor. This estimated temperature is only to be used as a guide as it can be influenced by deposits. This estimated temperature replaces the measured temperature in the output strings of the sensor so you will still see a temperature output from the sensor.

Note:

*   Only for Ex Sensors. Power Input: 240V Maximum AC Single Phase, this is exclusive for Ex sensor configuration and would be the best to protect the zener barriers used in the configuration, only single phase power supplies should be used.

Reference

Rheonics EX installation manual: https://rheonics.com/resources/manuals/
(https://rheonics.com/resources/manuals/)
Article: Is it still possible to measure viscosity without connecting the PT1000 to our SRV?
(https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000406648-is-it-still-possible-to-measure-viscosity-without-connecting-the-pt1000-to-our-srv-)
key_facts: The sensor estimates process temperature based on frequency characteristics, even without direct probe temperature measurement.
The estimated temperature is a guide and can be influenced by deposits.
This estimated temperature replaces the measured temperature in the sensor's output strings.
For Ex Sensors, a 240V Maximum AC Single Phase power input is recommended to best protect zener barriers.
Only single phase power supplies should be used for Ex Sensor configurations.
The Rheonics EX installation manual is available at https://rheonics.com/resources/manuals/.

An article titled 'Is it still possible to measure viscosity without connecting the PT1000 to our SRV?' is available at https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000406648-is-it-still-possible-to-measure-viscosity-without-connecting-the-pt1000-to-our-srv-.
warnings_notes: Note: Only for Ex Sensors. Power Input: 240V Maximum AC Single Phase, this is exclusive for Ex sensor configuration and would be the best to protect the zener barriers used in the configuration, only single phase power supplies should be used.
